g.data-narration: XOAR is a self-protecting software approach that breaks down monolithic applications into isolated compartments for cloud security.
g.text: XOAR: Self-Protecting Software for Cloud Security

g.data-narration: Traditional server applications like web servers run as large monolithic programs with extensive privileges, making them vulnerable to attacks.
In a web server, exploiting CGI handler gives attackers access to SSL keys, configs, and all server capabilities.
g.text: Web server with CGI, SSL, logging all in ONE process

g.data-narration: XOAR solves this by decomposing applications into isolated compartments, each with minimal privileges needed only for its function.
XOAR breaks monolithic applications into separate compartments: HTTP Parser, CGI Engine, SSL/TLS Handler, Logger, and Config Manager.
Each compartment receives only minimum permissions needed. CGI Engine cannot access SSL keys, and Logger cannot perform network operations.
g.text: No file write, no crypto keys

g.data-narration: The Compartment Manager orchestrates XOAR by creating compartments, initializing privileges, managing communication channels, and monitoring health.
The Policy Engine defines access control, communication, and privilege policies for each compartment in the XOAR system.
Compartments communicate through message passing, restricted shared memory, and system call filtering using seccomp on Linux.
XOAR provides reduced blast radius, smaller attack surface, easier auditing, better monitoring, and defense in depth for cloud applications.
g.text: Compromise one ≠ compromise all | Each compartment has focused code | Small compartments are reviewable | Update one without restarting all | Adds security layer at application level

g.data-narration: XOAR is ideal for cloud computing: it supports multi-tenant isolation, aligns with microservices, and complements VM and OS security.
XOAR adds application-level security that works alongside hypervisor isolation, kernel security, and network firewalls.
g.text: XOAR fills the gap: even with perfect VM & OS security, app vulnerabilities can cause data theft

g.data-narration: Compared to traditional monolithic apps, XOAR offers better security with smaller blast radius, though with added IPC complexity.
g.text: Single privilege level | Full compromise possible | Hard to isolate anomalies
Minimal privileges per compartment | Contained damage | Easy monitoring

g.data-narration: XOAR decomposes monolithic apps into isolated compartments with least privilege, limiting breach impact and providing defense in depth for cloud security.
g.text: ✓ XOAR decomposes monolithic apps into isolated, least-privilege compartments
✓ Core principles: Privilege Separation (decomposition) + Least Privilege (minimum permissions)
✓ Compromising one compartment cannot access capabilities of other isolated compartments
✓ Provides application-level defense in depth, complementing VM, OS, and network security

g.data-narration: XOAR is essential knowledge for cloud security, aligning with modern microservices and container architectures.
g.text: XOAR aligns with modern microservices and container architectures (Docker, Kubernetes)
